---
title: "factor_analysis"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "8/5/2025"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggraph)
library(igraph)
library(Hmisc)
library(writexl)

library(tidygraph)

```


#demographic
```{r}
food_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQA862.xlsx")
nutrition_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQB800.xlsx")
species = read_xlsx("data/species_866.xlsx")
metadata = read_xlsx("data/ethnicity_866.xlsx")

food_data <- food_data %>%
  mutate(across(c("Mushrooms", "Papaya", "Orange", "Chapati"), as.numeric))
food_data
nutrition_data
species
metadata
```

```{r}
colnames(species)[1] = "Participant_ID"
colnames(metadata)[1] = "Participant_ID"
threshold <- nrow(species) * 0.5

species_filtered1 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > nrow(species) * 0.2)
species_filtered1
species_filtered2 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > threshold)

#abundance_threshold <- 0.0001
#species_filtered2 <- species_filtered %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x) > (abundance_threshold * sum(species)))
#species_filtered2
species_filtered2 <- species_filtered2[, !names(species_filtered2) %in% c("unclassified", "others", "Homo sapiens")]
#species_filtered2$`Homo sapiens`
#species_filtered2
#food_data[is.na(food_data)] <- 0
#food_data <- data.frame(lapply(food_data, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
nutrition_data[is.na(nutrition_data)] <- 0
```


```{r}
food_data
nutrition_data
species_filtered2
species_filtered1
metadata
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(psych)
library(corrplot)
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)
library(gridExtra)
```


```{r}
# Assuming your data is already loaded as:
# food_data, species_filtered1, metadata

# Step 1: Merge datasets by Participant_ID
# Ensure all datasets have Participant_ID column
merged_data <- food_data %>%
  inner_join(species_filtered2, by = "Participant_ID") %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")

# Step 2: Prepare data for analysis
# Separate numeric variables (food and microbe data) from categorical (ethnicity)
numeric_cols <- merged_data %>%
  select(-Participant_ID, -FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
  select_if(is.numeric)

# Remove columns with near-zero variance or too many missing values
numeric_cols <- numeric_cols[, apply(numeric_cols, 2, function(x) var(x, na.rm = TRUE) > 0)]
numeric_cols <- numeric_cols[, colSums(is.na(numeric_cols)) < nrow(numeric_cols) * 0.5]

# Handle missing values (replace with median or remove rows)
numeric_cols_clean <- numeric_cols %>%
  mutate_all(~ifelse(is.na(.), median(., na.rm = TRUE), .))

# Step 3: Standardize the data (important for factor analysis)
numeric_scaled <- scale(numeric_cols_clean)
```


```{r}
# Step 4: Determine optimal number of factors
# Scree plot and parallel analysis
fa.parallel(numeric_scaled, fm = "pa", fa = "fa", n.iter = 10)

# Kaiser criterion (eigenvalues > 1)
eigenvalues <- eigen(cor(numeric_scaled))$values
n_factors <- sum(eigenvalues > 1)
cat("Number of factors with eigenvalues > 1:", n_factors, "\n")

# Step 5: Perform Factor Analysis
# You can adjust the number of factors (nfactors) based on Step 4 results
fa_result <- fa(numeric_scaled, 
                nfactors = min(5, n_factors), # Limit to 5 factors for interpretability
                rotate = "varimax", 
                fm = "pa")

print(fa_result)

# Step 6: Extract and visualize factor loadings
loadings_matrix <- fa_result$loadings
loadings_df <- as.data.frame(loadings_matrix[,])
loadings_df$Variable <- rownames(loadings_df)

# Create a heatmap of factor loadings
loadings_long <- loadings_df %>%
  pivot_longer(-Variable, names_to = "Factor", values_to = "Loading")

p1 <- ggplot(loadings_long, aes(x = Factor, y = Variable, fill = Loading)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", 
                       midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8)) +
  labs(title = "Factor Loadings Heatmap",
       x = "Factors", y = "Variables")

print(p1)

# Step 7: Calculate factor scores for each participant
factor_scores <- as.data.frame(fa_result$scores)
factor_scores$Participant_ID <- merged_data$Participant_ID
factor_scores$FREG5_Ethnic_Group <- merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group

# Step 8: Analyze relationship between factors and ethnicity
# ANOVA for each factor across ethnic groups
factor_ethnicity_results <- list()

for(i in 1:ncol(fa_result$scores)) {
  factor_name <- paste0("PA", i)
  formula_str <- paste(factor_name, "~ FREG5_Ethnic_Group")
  
  anova_result <- aov(as.formula(formula_str), data = factor_scores)
  factor_ethnicity_results[[factor_name]] <- summary(anova_result)
  
  cat("\n", factor_name, "vs Ethnicity:\n")
  print(summary(anova_result))
}

# Step 9: Visualize factor scores by ethnicity
# Box plots for each factor by ethnic group
plot_list <- list()

for(i in 1:ncol(fa_result$scores)) {
  factor_name <- paste0("PA", i)
  
  p <- ggplot(factor_scores, aes_string(x = "FREG5_Ethnic_Group", 
                                       y = factor_name, 
                                       fill = "FREG5_Ethnic_Group")) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "none") +
    labs(title = paste("Factor", i, "by Ethnicity"),
         x = "Ethnic Group", y = paste("Factor", i, "Score"))
  
  plot_list[[i]] <- p
}

# Display plots
grid.arrange(grobs = plot_list, ncol = 2)

# Step 10: Identify top contributing variables for each factor
top_loadings <- function(loadings_matrix, n_top = 10) {
  results <- list()
  
  for(i in 1:ncol(loadings_matrix)) {
    factor_loadings <- abs(loadings_matrix[, i])
    top_vars <- names(sort(factor_loadings, decreasing = TRUE)[1:n_top])
    top_values <- sort(factor_loadings, decreasing = TRUE)[1:n_top]
    
    results[[paste0("Factor_", i)]] <- data.frame(
      Variable = top_vars,
      Loading = top_values,
      Original_Loading = loadings_matrix[top_vars, i]
    )
    
    cat("\nTop contributors to Factor", i, ":\n")
    print(results[[paste0("Factor_", i)]])
  }
  
  return(results)
}

top_contributors <- top_loadings(loadings_matrix)

# Step 11: Alternative approach - PCA for comparison
pca_result <- PCA(numeric_scaled, graph = FALSE)

# Visualize PCA results
fviz_pca_var(pca_result, col.var = "contrib", 
             gradient.cols = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"),
             repel = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "PCA - Variable Contributions")

# Step 12: Create summary report
cat("\n=== FACTOR ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Number of participants:", nrow(factor_scores), "\n")
cat("Number of variables analyzed:", ncol(numeric_scaled), "\n")
cat("Number of factors extracted:", ncol(fa_result$scores), "\n")
cat("Proportion of variance explained:", sum(fa_result$Vaccounted[2,]), "\n")

# Correlation between factors and ethnicity (if ethnicity is numeric)
if(is.numeric(as.numeric(as.factor(factor_scores$FREG5_Ethnic_Group)))) {
  ethnicity_numeric <- as.numeric(as.factor(factor_scores$FREG5_Ethnic_Group))
  factor_correlations <- cor(factor_scores[,1:ncol(fa_result$scores)], 
                            ethnicity_numeric)
  cat("\nCorrelations between factors and ethnicity:\n")
  print(factor_correlations)
}
```


```{r}


# Step 1: Merge datasets by Participant_ID
# Ensure all datasets have Participant_ID column
merged_data <- food_data %>%
  inner_join(species_filtered2, by = "Participant_ID") %>%
  inner_join(metadata, by = "Participant_ID")

# Step 2: Prepare data for analysis
# Separate numeric variables (food and microbe data) from categorical (ethnicity)
numeric_cols <- merged_data %>%
  select(-Participant_ID, -FREG5_Ethnic_Group) %>%
  select_if(is.numeric)

# Remove columns with near-zero variance or too many missing values
numeric_cols <- numeric_cols[, apply(numeric_cols, 2, function(x) var(x, na.rm = TRUE) > 0)]
numeric_cols <- numeric_cols[, colSums(is.na(numeric_cols)) < nrow(numeric_cols) * 0.5]

# Handle missing values (replace with median or remove rows)
numeric_cols_clean <- numeric_cols %>%
  mutate_all(~ifelse(is.na(.), median(., na.rm = TRUE), .))

# Step 3: Standardize the data (important for factor analysis)
numeric_scaled <- scale(numeric_cols_clean)

# Step 4: Determine optimal number of factors
# Method 1: Kaiser criterion (eigenvalues > 1) - Very fast
eigenvalues <- eigen(cor(numeric_scaled))$values
n_factors_kaiser <- sum(eigenvalues > 1)
cat("Kaiser criterion - Number of factors with eigenvalues > 1:", n_factors_kaiser, "\n")

# Method 2: Scree plot (visual inspection) - Fast
plot(eigenvalues, type = "b", main = "Scree Plot", 
     xlab = "Factor Number", ylab = "Eigenvalue")
abline(h = 1, col = "red", lty = 2)

# Method 3: Variance explained threshold - Fast
cumvar <- cumsum(eigenvalues) / sum(eigenvalues)
n_factors_80 <- which(cumvar >= 0.80)[1]  # 80% variance explained
cat("Factors explaining 80% variance:", n_factors_80, "\n")

# Method 4: Fast parallel analysis (reduced iterations) - Much faster
# Only run if you have time, otherwise skip
if(ncol(numeric_scaled) < 100) {  # Only for smaller datasets
  cat("Running fast parallel analysis...\n")
  fa_parallel_fast <- fa.parallel(numeric_scaled, fm = "pa", fa = "fa", 
                                  n.iter = 20, show.legend = FALSE)  # Reduced iterations
  n_factors_parallel <- fa_parallel_fast$nfact
  cat("Parallel analysis suggests:", n_factors_parallel, "factors\n")
} else {
  cat("Skipping parallel analysis for large dataset\n")
  n_factors_parallel <- n_factors_kaiser
}

# Choose number of factors (you can adjust this logic)
n_factors <- min(n_factors_kaiser, 8)  # Limit to 8 factors max for interpretability
cat("Selected number of factors:", n_factors, "\n")

# Step 5: Perform Factor Analysis
# You can adjust the number of factors (nfactors) based on Step 4 results
fa_result <- fa(numeric_scaled, 
                nfactors = n_factors, 
                rotate = "varimax", 
                fm = "pa")

print(fa_result)

# Step 6: Extract and visualize factor loadings
loadings_matrix <- fa_result$loadings
loadings_df <- as.data.frame(loadings_matrix[,])
loadings_df$Variable <- rownames(loadings_df)

# Create a heatmap of factor loadings
loadings_long <- loadings_df %>%
  pivot_longer(-Variable, names_to = "Factor", values_to = "Loading")

p1 <- ggplot(loadings_long, aes(x = Factor, y = Variable, fill = Loading)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", 
                       midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8)) +
  labs(title = "Factor Loadings Heatmap",
       x = "Factors", y = "Variables")

print(p1)

# Step 7: Calculate factor scores for each participant
factor_scores <- as.data.frame(fa_result$scores)
factor_scores$Participant_ID <- merged_data$Participant_ID
factor_scores$FREG5_Ethnic_Group <- merged_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group

# Step 8: Analyze relationship between factors and ethnicity
# ANOVA for each factor across ethnic groups
factor_ethnicity_results <- list()

for(i in 1:ncol(fa_result$scores)) {
  factor_name <- paste0("PA", i)
  formula_str <- paste(factor_name, "~ FREG5_Ethnic_Group")
  
  anova_result <- aov(as.formula(formula_str), data = factor_scores)
  factor_ethnicity_results[[factor_name]] <- summary(anova_result)
  
  cat("\n", factor_name, "vs Ethnicity:\n")
  print(summary(anova_result))
}

# Step 9: Visualize factor scores by ethnicity
# Box plots for each factor by ethnic group
plot_list <- list()

for(i in 1:ncol(fa_result$scores)) {
  factor_name <- paste0("PA", i)
  
  p <- ggplot(factor_scores, aes_string(x = "FREG5_Ethnic_Group", 
                                       y = factor_name, 
                                       fill = "FREG5_Ethnic_Group")) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "none") +
    labs(title = paste("Factor", i, "by Ethnicity"),
         x = "Ethnic Group", y = paste("Factor", i, "Score"))
  
  plot_list[[i]] <- p
}

# Display plots
grid.arrange(grobs = plot_list, ncol = 2)

# Step 10: Identify top contributing variables for each factor
top_loadings <- function(loadings_matrix, n_top = 10) {
  results <- list()
  
  for(i in 1:ncol(loadings_matrix)) {
    factor_loadings <- abs(loadings_matrix[, i])
    top_vars <- names(sort(factor_loadings, decreasing = TRUE)[1:n_top])
    top_values <- sort(factor_loadings, decreasing = TRUE)[1:n_top]
    
    results[[paste0("Factor_", i)]] <- data.frame(
      Variable = top_vars,
      Loading = top_values,
      Original_Loading = loadings_matrix[top_vars, i]
    )
    
    cat("\nTop contributors to Factor", i, ":\n")
    print(results[[paste0("Factor_", i)]])
  }
  
  return(results)
}

top_contributors <- top_loadings(loadings_matrix)

# Step 11: Alternative approach - PCA for comparison
pca_result <- PCA(numeric_scaled, graph = FALSE)

# Visualize PCA results
fviz_pca_var(pca_result, col.var = "contrib", 
             gradient.cols = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"),
             repel = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "PCA - Variable Contributions")

# Step 12: Create summary report
cat("\n=== FACTOR ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Number of participants:", nrow(factor_scores), "\n")
cat("Number of variables analyzed:", ncol(numeric_scaled), "\n")
cat("Number of factors extracted:", ncol(fa_result$scores), "\n")
cat("Proportion of variance explained:", sum(fa_result$Vaccounted[2,]), "\n")

# Correlation between factors and ethnicity (if ethnicity is numeric)
if(is.numeric(as.numeric(as.factor(factor_scores$FREG5_Ethnic_Group)))) {
  ethnicity_numeric <- as.numeric(as.factor(factor_scores$FREG5_Ethnic_Group))
  factor_correlations <- cor(factor_scores[,1:ncol(fa_result$scores)], 
                            ethnicity_numeric)
  cat("\nCorrelations between factors and ethnicity:\n")
  print(factor_correlations)
}
```

```{r}
# Look at PA5's top loadings
top_contributors$Factor_5  # From your analysis

# Statistical significance
ethnicity_numeric <- as.numeric(as.factor(factor_scores$FREG5_Ethnic_Group))
cor.test(factor_scores$PA5, ethnicity_numeric)

# Which ethnic groups have highest/lowest PA5 scores
aggregate(PA5 ~ FREG5_Ethnic_Group, data = factor_scores, mean)
```
